page: Alibaba Cloud ecs.c8ine.xlarge is an x86_64 architecture server instance powered by the KVM hypervisor. It features 4 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 4 physical cores of an Intel Xeon processor from the SPR family, running at a base clock speed of 2.7 GHz. With a 1.0 thread-per-core configuration, each vCPU maps directly to a physical core without hyperthreading, ensuring dedicated execution resources.

The instance is equipped with 8.0 GB of system memory, yielding a resource density of 2.0 GB per core. It does not include local storage, meaning all storage requirements must be met via network-attached block storage. Network connectivity is supported by a baseline bandwidth of 7 Gbps.

Given its 1:2 vCPU-to-RAM ratio and dedicated physical cores, this instance is designed for compute-intensive workloads that require predictable CPU performance without resource sharing. The lack of local storage and moderate memory footprint make it a cost-efficient choice for distributed compute nodes, microservices, and lightweight web serving where local disk I/O is not a primary bottleneck.
description: Alibaba Cloud ecs.c8ine.xlarge is an x86_64 compute-optimized server instance running on the KVM hypervisor. It is configured with 4 dedicated vCPUs mapped directly to 4 physical cores of an Intel Xeon SPR processor operating at 2.7 GHz. Because it utilizes a 1.0 thread-per-core configuration, it provides dedicated processing power without hyperthreading. The instance includes 8.0 GB of system memory, resulting in a resource density of 2.0 GB per core, and features no local storage, relying entirely on network-attached storage. It delivers a baseline network bandwidth of 7 Gbps. This hardware profile makes the instance a cost-efficient option for compute-bound workloads, such as distributed processing, microservices, and web serving, where predictable CPU execution is required and local storage is not necessary.
